title: Fix admin Unverified Origin login error, press kit text content display, and password dialog

requirements.summary: Fix the 'Unverified origin' error on the /admin route when attempting Internet Identity login in the deployed environment by ensuring the InternetIdentityProvider is configured with the correct II URL (https://identity.ic0.app) and derivationOrigin matching the deployed frontend canister URL
acceptanceCriteria: Navigating to /admin and clicking the Internet Identity login button no longer produces an 'Unverified origin' error
The Internet Identity flow completes and the authenticated principal is returned to the admin page
The derivationOrigin passed to the II provider matches the deployed frontend canister URL
The II provider URL is set to https://identity.ic0.app for production

file_operations.description: Update the InternetIdentityProvider configuration to use the correct II URL (https://identity.ic0.app) for production and set the derivationOrigin to the deployed frontend canister URL. Ensure the provider URL and derivationOrigin are dynamically determined based on the deployment environment (local vs production). The derivationOrigin should be the frontend canister URL in the format https://<canister-id>.icp0.io for deployed environments.

Review and ensure the Internet Identity login flow in AdminDashboard correctly uses the configured InternetIdentityProvider from main.tsx. Verify that no additional provider configuration is overriding the main configuration. Ensure error handling displays actionable messages if the II flow fails.

requirements.summary: Fix the press kit page so that all CMS text content (about text, features list, game details, social links) is correctly fetched from the backend and rendered in the PressKitPage by investigating and fixing the useQueries hooks and ensuring the anonymous actor is properly initialized for public reads
acceptanceCriteria: The About text section displays the text saved via the CMS
The Features list section displays the features saved via the CMS
The Game Details section displays the details saved via the CMS
Social links saved in the CMS are rendered in the Social Media section
Text content is fetched using the anonymous actor (no authentication required for public reads)
React Query hooks for text fields return data without errors in the deployed environment

file_operations.description: Audit all React Query hooks that fetch text-based content (about text, features, game details, social links). Ensure query keys are correct and consistent. Verify that the anonymous actor is properly passed and used for public read queries. Check that the enabled flag correctly waits for the actor to be available. Add error logging to identify any backend call failures or data structure mismatches.

Review how PressKitPage consumes the text data from useQueries hooks. Ensure that content fields (aboutText, features, gameDetails, social links) are correctly extracted from query results and passed to child components. Verify that loading states and data availability checks do not prevent text from rendering. Add fallback handling for empty or null text fields to ensure visibility during debugging.

Verify that the anonymous actor is correctly initialized for unauthenticated public reads. Ensure the actor is available immediately on mount without requiring authentication. Check that the actor creation logic does not fail silently for anonymous contexts. Confirm that dependent queries are notified when the anonymous actor becomes available.

requirements.summary: Fix the password gate dialog (PasswordGateModal) on the press kit page by investigating the verifyPassword backend call, ensuring the anonymous actor is available and correctly invoked, that sessionStorage verification state is set on success, and that the modal dismisses correctly
acceptanceCriteria: The PasswordGateModal renders and is interactable when password protection is enabled
Submitting the correct password calls the backend verifyPassword method without error
On successful verification, sessionStorage is updated and the modal closes
On incorrect password, an inline error message is displayed
The modal does not crash or silently fail when the actor is in anonymous/unauthenticated context

file_operations.description: Audit the verifyPassword backend call inside PasswordGateModal. Ensure the anonymous actor is retrieved using useActor and is available before attempting the verification call. Verify that the actor is not undefined or null when the password is submitted. Check that sessionStorage is correctly updated with the verification state key on successful password verification. Ensure the modal's onClose callback is invoked to dismiss the modal after successful verification. Add error handling and logging for actor unavailability, network errors, or backend rejections. Display inline error messages for incorrect passwords or failed verification attempts.

Review the integration of PasswordGateModal in PressKitPage. Ensure the modal is conditionally rendered based on password protection status and sessionStorage verification state. Verify that the modal receives the correct props and callbacks. Confirm that the modal does not get unmounted prematurely or fail to re-render after verification state changes. Check that the password protection status query is correctly fetched and passed to the modal logic.
